title: The Echo Chamber
desc: A long-range Mugic relay needs testing. Bring earplugs.

steps.text:
Deep in Castle Bodhran's foundation is a room shaped like the inside of a bell. The Echo Chamber — used for long-range Mugic communication across vast distances. A muge stands at the center, preparing a test transmission.

"I need someone at the far wall to confirm reception. When I send the tone, you should feel it in your teeth before you hear it. If you feel it in your bones instead, run."

She doesn't explain why. She hands you a pair of wax earplugs.

options.label: Take position at the far wall — with earplugs
Ask what 'bones instead of teeth' means

steps.text:
You press yourself against the far wall. The stone vibrates faintly, residual echoes from centuries of transmissions trapped in the rock.

The muge sings a single note. It hits the chamber walls and multiplies — one note becomes seven, becomes forty-nine, becomes a cascading waterfall of harmonics that converge on your position.

Teeth. You feel it in your teeth. Good.

You give a thumbs up. The muge adjusts her pitch and sends a second tone. This one is warmer, lower. You feel it in your jaw, your sinuses, behind your eyes. It's uncomfortable but not alarming.

"Clear signal," she announces. "The relay is functional. Strongest reception in months." She looks at you with professional approval. "You have good teeth. Come back if you want steady work."

options.label: Appreciate the strangest job offer you've received

steps.text:
"It means the chamber's resonance has shifted from communication frequency to demolition frequency. The last time that happened, it shook three floors of the castle loose."

You put the earplugs in immediately.

The test goes smoothly. The muge's note fills the chamber with clean, clear harmonics. You feel it in your teeth — nowhere else. Each successive tone is precisely controlled, bouncing off the walls in predictable patterns.

But on the seventh tone, something shifts. The echo doesn't die — it builds, layering on itself. The muge's eyes widen. She cuts the note short and sings a sharp counter-frequency. The building echo collapses into silence.

"That," she says into the quiet, "is why we test." She marks something on her chart. "Seventh harmonic is unstable. I'll need to recalibrate before we send anything real."

You're glad the earplugs were good.

options.label: Leave while your hearing is intact
